SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-03-2011 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up on the record. (No Case File sent to Dept. 514)
Dept. 514, Judge: Tomar Mason, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: B. Fields, #7235

Williams Weisberg, Esq. for and with the Plaintiff Julie Boyard.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff and against defendant Manuel G. Ayan for Judgment as follows:
(Request for Punitive damages is taken under advisement), all other damgaes are awarded.

After further reviewed, the Court denied the award for Punitive Damages.

Damges $244,637.33
Prejudgment Interest $827.89
Costs $1,534.78
Unpaid Sanction $3,000.00
Total 250,000.00
Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JULIE BAYARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES; COMPEL DEPOSITION ATTENDANCE; DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED; COMPEL MONETARY SANCTIONS.
Appearances: William R. Glenn, III (987.2900) for plaintiff and moving party; Manuel Ayan (706.6666) defendant and responding party appearing in propria persona.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted, no opposition filed. Plaintiff to provide a copy of the interrogatories to defendant, which must be responded to by September 16, 2011. Defendant to appear for deposition by September 16, 2011. Sanctions of $1500 to be paid by defendant to plaintiff's counsel by November 1, 2011. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

Appearances: William R. Glenn, III (987.2900) for plaintiff Julie Bayard; Elissa Lasserre of the Law Offices of Jon P. Rankin (435.5822) counsel for defendant and moving to be relieved.
No other appearances. The court granted the motion conditioned on Mr. Rankin's office providing plaintiff's counsel with the contact information for Mr. Ayan. Order signed. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

THE ANSWER MUST SET FORTH FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A DEFENSE(S). AS TO THE 12TH AND 13TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.
